subject: [PATCH 8/8] nfsd: hold net namespace reference in nfsd_file

reviews.author: Al Viro
reviews.summary: Reviewer Al Viro noted that the patch introduces a potential scalability issue due to shared cachelines between nfsd_file_alloc() and nfsd_file_free(), which may have implications for kernel performance on systems with net namespace enabled.
reviews.raw_body:
That means that each nfsd_file_alloc()/nfsd_file_free() is now touching
the same cacheline on kernels with netns enabled.  Scalability implications
might be interesting...

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged a concern about releasing the net namespace reference in nfsd_file_free() and agreed to explore alternative solutions.
reviews.raw_body:
That's a very good point.

There are some alternatives. Let me play around with them and see if I
can find something better.

Thanks for the review,
-- 
Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged a valid concern about excessive get/put_net activity, agreed to implement a solution in v2 by only taking net references for GC'ed files.
reviews.raw_body:
That's definitely a valid concern. I looked at a couple of alternatives
but they turned out to be pretty nasty.

One thing we can do is only take a net reference for GC'ed files, since
we don't need a reference for the others. That would cut down some of
the get/put_net activity, but it's still likely to be substantial. I'll
plan to implement that for v2.

I'll keep thinking about other ways to do this, but I think we're stuck
taking net references for at least some of these for now.
-- 
Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>

2026-06-01.patch_summary: This patch fixes a use-after-free bug in the NFS daemon by holding a reference to the net namespace for each file. The fix involves taking a net-namespace reference when allocating an nfsd_file and releasing it when freeing the file. This ensures that the net namespace is always valid, even if the file is isolated from the hash table and LRU. The patch also updates the nfsd_file_put_local function to release the net namespace reference before dropping the file reference.
